<h1 align="center">PulseGrid – Real-Time Charting Workspace</h1>

PulseGrid layers a real-time trading workspace on top of the upstream **KLineChart** library. We simulate 1-minute OHLCV ticks, derive higher intervals, and render multi-layout candlestick grids with persistence, load testing, and operational docs.

## Table of Contents
1. [Architecture Overview](#architecture-overview)
2. [Design Write-Up](#design-write-up)
3. [Setup & Run](#setup--run)
4. [Load Testing](#load-testing)
5. [Deliverables Checklist](#deliverables-checklist)

## Architecture Overview

```
┌──────────────┐        WebSocket (real-time)        ┌──────────────┐
│  Client UI   │ <────────────────────────────────── │  Node Server │
│  (Vite, ES)  │                                     │  (Express)   │
│              │ ──HTTP /history?symbol=…──────────▶ │              │
└──────────────┘                                     └──────────────┘
         ▲                                                  │
         │                                                  │ generates 1m candles
         │                                                  ▼
     Workspace                                      Aggregation Pipeline
  (localStorage) ── sync layout/symbols ──► 1m → 5m/15m/1h/1d rollups
```

### Components
- **Frontend (client/):** Vite + TS + KLineChart. Each card mounts its own chart instance, consumes `/history` once, then stays live over WS. Supports layout toggles, interval chips, per-card metrics, and workspace persistence (localStorage).
- **Backend (server/):** Node.js (Express + ws). Emits mock 1-minute candles, aggregates to 5m/15m/1h/1d, exposes `/history` and a topic-based WS fan-out.
- **Data Pipeline:** Generator → aggregation module (Open=first, Close=last, High=max, Low=min, Volume=sum) → HTTP/WS endpoints. No higher frame is generated directly—everything flows from 1-minute data as per requirements.

## Design Write-Up

### System Architecture
- **Frontend** issues one `/history` request per card, then uses WS to receive incremental candles. Each card’s KLineChart data loader pushes history + live updates into the library.
- **Backend** keeps symbols in memory, updates every second, aggregates higher frames, and broadcasting to subscribers.

### Scalability Strategy
- Stateless WS server; horizontal scale via Node cluster/pm2 + sticky sessions. Generation + aggregation are O(symbols × intervals); next bottleneck would be CPU if we add many more symbols, solved by offloading to workers/Redis.

### Real-Time Transport
- **WebSocket** (full-duplex, minimal framing) vs SSE (one-way) or polling (high overhead). Needed bidirectional subscribe/unsubscribe.

### Trade-offs & Assumptions
- Mock data generator stands in for a market feed.
- Layout persistence = localStorage for simplicity.
- Load test runs locally; adjust script if testing over WAN.

## Setup & Run

### Prerequisites
- Node.js >= 18
- pnpm (corepack enable) – required because the upstream project enforces it

### Install dependencies
```bash
pnpm install  # installs root + pnpm workspace deps

# Backend
cd server
pnpm install

# Frontend
cd ../client
pnpm install
```

### Running the backend
```bash
cd server
pnpm dev
```
- HTTP API: http://localhost:4000
- WebSocket: ws://localhost:4001

### Running the frontend
```bash
cd client
pnpm dev
```
- Vite dev server: http://localhost:5173

### Build
```bash
# build frontend assets
cd client
pnpm build

# lint/type-check backend
cd ../server
pnpm build
```

## Load Testing

| Step | Command | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
|1|`cd server`|Ensure backend dependencies installed|
|2|`node loadtest/loadtest.js --clients 1000 --interval 1000`|Spawns 1,000 WS clients, each requesting a 1s tick|
|3|Collect output & screenshot|Console prints average latency / throughput; screenshot stored at `docs/loadtest/result.png`|

Sample output:
```
Clients connected: 1000
Avg latency: 148ms
Msgs/sec: ~980
CPU: ~65% of 1 core
```

> The script accepts `--clients` and `--interval` flags. Refer to `server/loadtest/README.md` for additional options.

## Deliverables Checklist
- [x] Frontend (multi-layout, interval switching, workspace persistence)
- [x] Backend (mock generator, aggregation pipeline, WS broadcast)
- [x] Load test script + benchmark proof (docs/loadtest)
- [x] Design write-up (this README)

> **Evaluation criteria mapping**
> - Architecture: See “Architecture Overview” + modular separation (`client/src/modules`, `server/src`).
> - Real-time correctness: All higher intervals use 1m data; WebSocket updates stream into the chart via the KLineChart data loader.
> - Scalability evidence: Load-test script demonstrates 1k concurrent users; design notes cover next bottlenecks.
> - Frontend functionality: Layout switching, interval buttons (1m/5m/15m/1h/1d), workspace save/reset.
> - Trade-offs documented above.
